question_text: Two objects, A and B, have the same volume but object A has more mass than object B. Which statement is true?

options.label: A
options.text: Object A is less dense and will float higher in water

options.label: B
options.text: Object A is denser and will sink below object B in water

options.label: C
options.text: Both objects have the same density because their volumes are equal

options.label: D
options.text: Object B is denser because it has less mass

explanation: Since both have the same volume but A has more mass, object A must be denser (density = mass ÷ volume). Higher density means it will sink further in water compared to object B.
